Off Calendar Aug-25-2021 Continued To Oct-20-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Margaret Greene appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

Ms. Greene stated that she is looking for an attorney and that she thinks it may take about 30-days. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to October 20, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Vedica Puri. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-10-2021 Continued To Mar-10-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Margaret Greene appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that there is no response to the Probate Examiner's notes that were issued in November. Ms. Greene stated that she filed proof of publication on November 25th, and that she filed a receipt and a clip of the newspaper. The Court stated that it doesn't say where this was published, that it needs to know in what circulation, that it needs to have an attestation and noted that the duties and liabilities form has been filed, but does not have the confidential attachment. Ms. Greene stated that they just sent her a receipt, that it was published on November 24th and that she put the confidential document in the drop box. The Court stated that the petition still has some missing information (5.b. and item 8) that needs to be provided in writing and asked Ms. Greene if she is the Trustee for the trust. Ms. Greene responded "yes" and that she is the first choice and then her Sister. The Court informed Ms. Greene that all of the Beneficiaries of the trust need to be served with this petition and that she needs to provide all information regarding debts related to the estate. Ms. Greene stated that all of the Beneficiaries have been served and that she is still thinking about whether or not she will be getting an attorney. The Court explained how difficult probate is, and stated that there are specific requirements and that it needs to make sure that the appointed person can handle the estate effectively. Ms. Greene needs to file a declaration answering 5.b. indicating who the Trustee is, who the Beneficiaries are, that they have been noticed and provide a proposed order. The Court also informed Ms. Greene that she will not be allowed to serve without a bond, that she needs a $20,000.00 bond with limited authority, or a full bond with full authority and that it is up to her. Ms. Greene stated that there is a mortgage, that debts are accumulating right now and that there are property taxes. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to March 10, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Greene is to file her declaration and provide the proposed order by March 3, 2021.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
